intro: Asperula oetaea, also known as Greek woodruff, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The health benefits of Asperula oetaea include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Asperula oetaea is commonly used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
